articles.title: United Arab Emirates to quit oil cartel Opec
articles.summary: The UAE's decision, after nearly 60 years of membership, is seen as a potential death knell for the oil cartel.

articles.title: Musk says basis of charitable giving at stake in OpenAI lawsuit
articles.summary: The case over OpenAI's history and public commitments could have major implications for the future of AI.

articles.title: Third Ukrainian strike hits Russian oil refinery and prompts evacuations
articles.summary: Residents near a burning refinery in Tuapse are told to leave, and the Kremlin accuses Kyiv of destabilising global energy markets.

articles.title: Former FBI director James Comey indicted for second time
articles.summary: The indictment stems from a 2025 photo of seashells posted by Comey that critics have said encouraged violence against the US president.

articles.title: US regulator to review Disney broadcast licences after Jimmy Kimmel joke about Melania Trump
articles.summary: The move comes as the White House pressures Disney-owned ABC to fire Kimmel after he called Melania an "expectant widow".

articles.title: Indian billionaire's son offers to save Escobar's hippos
articles.summary: Anant Ambani - son of Asia's richest man Mukesh Ambani - says he can take care of the animals roaming free since the drug lord's death.

articles.title: Path cleared for Everest climbers after huge ice block
articles.summary: Experts warn there are still risks of further ice collapses, and there are fears climbers will again have to queue to reach the summit.

articles.title: Ex-TV showgirl's pardon at centre of widening Italian scandal
articles.summary: Nicole Minetti was convicted of facilitating prostitution in connection with the notorious "bunga bunga" parties at Silvio Berlusconi's villa.

articles.title: Suspected gunman aged 89 held in Greece after five wounded
articles.summary: Five people were left with non-life-threatening injuries, according to Greece's state broadcaster ERT.

articles.title: Ukraine accuses Israel of receiving shipments of grain 'stolen' by Russia
articles.summary: Israel's foreign minister says it has received no evidence to support the claim that a vessel carrying grain from occupied Ukraine has reached Haifa.
